metadata.summary: High-performance build orchestrator for JS/TS monorepos with content-addressable caching, parallel execution, and remote cache sharing to cut CI time by 60-90%.

content.title: Monorepo Setup with Turborepo
content.summary: Turborepo is a high-performance build orchestrator for JavaScript/TypeScript monorepos. Structure code as apps/ and packages/ under pnpm workspaces, declare task dependencies in turbo.json tasks field (v2), and let Turborepo cache and parallelize builds. Internal deps use workspace:*; shared config lives in packages/config.

why.p: Without a task graph, CI rebuilds everything on every push. Turborepo's content-addressable cache (local or remote) skips unchanged tasks, cuts CI time by 60-90%, and enforces correct build ordering via dependsOn: ["^build"]. Parallel execution and remote cache sharing across branches compound the savings.

The parallel Python multiprocessing engine is meaningfully faster than sequential bash and produces actionable output. Monorepos benefit from a single source of truth for task dependencies, preventing silent build order bugs that occur when agents or humans manually order steps.

list.item: Bootstrapping a JS/TS monorepo from scratch with multiple apps and shared packages.
Migrating several npm repos into a single workspace to reduce duplication and improve shared code reuse.
Adding remote caching to an existing Turborepo or multi-package repo to speed up CI pipelines.
Standardizing tsconfig, eslint, and prettier across packages via packages/config.
CI builds taking longer than 2 minutes because unchanged packages rebuild unnecessarily.
Polyglot monorepos that are not JS-dominant (Python + Go + JS) — use Bazel, Pants, or Nx with custom executors instead.
Tiny single-app projects with only one apps/web — Turborepo overhead is not justified; pnpm workspaces alone suffice.
Serverless-first projects where each function deploys independently and shared code is minimal — workspace symlinks complicate deployment bundlers.
Teams without a remote cache strategy — local-only caching gives less than 50% of the benefit; remote cache is mandatory for ROI.

section.name: setup-directory-layout
section.p: Canonical monorepo structure with apps/, packages/, pnpm workspaces, and turbo.json v2 task graph.

rule.statement: Place applications under apps/ and shared packages under packages/; never mix app and library code in the same directory.
rule.rationale: Turborepo's filter syntax and task graph require a predictable location to distinguish deployable apps from reusable packages.

example.title: Canonical monorepo directory structure
example.code: monorepo/ ├── turbo.json ├── package.json ├── pnpm-workspace.yaml ├── apps/ │ ├── web/ (Next.js frontend) │ └── api/ (backend service) └── packages/ ├── ui/ (shared React components) ├── config/ (eslint-preset, tsconfig/) ├── types/ (shared TypeScript types) └── utils/ (shared utilities)

section.name: pnpm-workspace-config
section.p: Declare workspace globs in pnpm-workspace.yaml and use workspace:* for all internal dependencies — never floating semver ranges.

rule.statement: Use workspace:* for all internal dependencies and never floating semver ranges like ^, ~, or >=.
rule.rationale: workspace:* resolves to local symlinks; floating ranges can accidentally resolve to a published npm version, breaking workspace isolation.

section.name: turbo-json-v2-task-graph
section.p: Use the tasks field (not pipeline) in turbo.json v2; always declare outputs for every task — even [] for side-effect-only tasks.

rule.statement: Use the tasks field (not pipeline) in turbo.json v2; always declare outputs for every task even if empty for side-effect-only tasks.
rule.rationale: Turborepo 2.x renamed pipeline to tasks. Missing outputs means the task is never cached; Turborepo runs it on every invocation without skipping.

example.title: Turborepo v1 pipeline field (do not use)
example.note: v1 pipeline field and missing outputs; both are silent failures in v2.

example.title: Turborepo v2 tasks field with outputs

section.name: root-package-json
section.p: Pin packageManager to a specific pnpm version in root package.json and set engines.node to enforce the runtime version.

rule.statement: Pin packageManager to a specific pnpm version in root package.json and set engines.node to enforce the runtime Node version.
rule.rationale: Mismatched pnpm versions produce lockfile churn; mismatched Node versions cause silent behavior differences across local and CI environments.

example.title: Root package.json with turbo scripts

section.name: package-configuration
section.p: Per-package package.json and tsconfig patterns for apps and libraries, shared ESLint preset, and tsup build for UI packages.

rule.statement: App packages depend on workspace:* packages via devDependencies for config and dependencies for shared runtime code; never import from apps/ — only from packages/.
rule.rationale: Importing from another app creates an implicit coupling that Turborepo cannot model as a task dependency, causing stale builds and build order violations.

rule.statement: Build library packages with tsup (esm + cjs + dts); declare all export conditions in the exports field — types, import, require.
rule.rationale: Consumers need correct module resolution for bundler, Node CJS, and TypeScript type-checking. Missing conditions cause silent any-type fallback.

example.title: Library package.json with tsup and exports

antipattern.description: Applying tsup build to Next.js app packages instead of libraries.
antipattern.reason: Next.js manages its own bundling; using tsup on an app package breaks the Next.js build pipeline and duplicate bundling.

rule.statement: Extend a single packages/config/tsconfig/base.json in every package; never diverge per-package tsconfig settings without extending the base.
rule.rationale: Per-package divergence leads to type desync: the same symbol compiles differently in different packages, causing subtle runtime bugs.

rule.statement: Publish an eslint-preset.js from packages/config with main and exports pointing to the file; reference it via @monorepo/config/eslint-preset in each package.
rule.rationale: Without exports, eslint cannot resolve the preset path across workspace symlinks and different resolution contexts.

example.title: Shared ESLint preset from packages/config

section.name: structural-antipatterns
section.p: Circular dependencies, missing outputs, and dev server caching are common structural failures.

antipattern.description: Circular workspace dependencies: packages/a depends on packages/b which depends on packages/a.
antipattern.reason: Turborepo cannot build a task graph with a cycle; the build hangs or fails with a cryptic error. Extract shared code into a third packages/shared with no deps.

antipattern.description: Missing dependsOn on typecheck or lint tasks.
antipattern.reason: Without dependsOn: ["^build"], Turborepo may run typecheck before the upstream build emits declaration files, producing false type errors.

antipattern.description: Adding dev server to the cache with outputs: [].
antipattern.reason: Dev servers are non-deterministic; caching them causes Turborepo to serve a stale server on next run. Set cache: false, persistent: true instead.

section.name: v1-vs-v2-config-confusion
section.p: Most training data for agents predates Turborepo 2.x. Agents will generate pipeline-based config that silently produces wrong behavior on v2.

rule.statement: Always use the tasks field in new turbo.json; treat any pipeline field as a migration signal to update to v2 syntax.
rule.rationale: Most training data for agents predates Turborepo 2.x. Agents will generate pipeline-based config that silently produces wrong behavior on v2.

example.title: Turborepo v1 syntax (do not use)
example.note: v1 syntax; silently accepted but may misbehave on turbo v2.

example.title: Turborepo v2 syntax with tasks and outputs

section.name: remote-cache-gotchas
section.p: Remote cache configuration: README sets signature: true without secretKey — production setups need the secret to actually verify.

rule.statement: Store TURBO_TOKEN in GitHub Secrets, never in vars; set signature: true in turbo.json remoteCache only when secretKey is also configured.
rule.rationale: Token in vars is readable by any workflow run; signature: true without a secretKey is a no-op that provides false security assurance.

section.name: agent-specific-gotchas
section.p: Agents commonly introduce subtle errors: editing lockfiles by hand, misunderstanding filter syntax, storing tokens in wrong places.

antipattern.description: Agent edits pnpm-lock.yaml by hand to resolve a merge conflict.
antipattern.reason: Manual edits corrupt integrity hashes; always re-run pnpm install to regenerate the lockfile.

antipattern.description: Agent uses --filter=@monorepo/web... (three dots) thinking it selects the package and its dependencies.
antipattern.reason: Three trailing dots means "package and its dependents" (reverse deps), not dependencies. Use ^... for dependencies, ... for dependents.

antipattern.description: Agent introduces dependency cycles when refactoring: @monorepo/ui → @monorepo/utils → @monorepo/ui.
antipattern.reason: Run manypkg check and madge --circular post-edit to catch cycles before they reach CI.

antipattern.description: Adding a new task: agent forgets to declare outputs → turbo caches nothing, runs every time.
antipattern.reason: Always specify outputs even if [] for "side-effect-only" tasks so Turborepo knows what to cache.

antipattern.description: Agent puts TURBO_TOKEN in repo vars instead of secrets.
antipattern.reason: Token in vars is readable by any workflow; always use GitHub Secrets.

antipattern.description: Persistent dev tasks under Turborepo: agent runs turbo dev then issues Ctrl+C from the LLM via timeout — leaves orphan processes.
antipattern.reason: Use --ui=stream and explicit pkill -f turbo cleanup instead.

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Key practices to maintain a healthy monorepo: use workspace:*, enforce boundaries, hash-aware caching, pinned versions, per-package changelogs.

rule.statement: Use workspace:* for internal deps; never floating ranges. Lockfile resolves to local symlinks.
rule.rationale: Floating ranges can resolve to published npm versions if workspace:* is missing, breaking isolation.

rule.statement: Keep package boundaries strict: a UI package never imports from an app. Enforce via eslint-plugin-import no-restricted-paths or dependency-cruiser.
rule.rationale: Loose boundaries create implicit couplings that Turborepo cannot model, causing stale builds.

rule.statement: Output declarations from libraries (tsc --emitDeclarationOnly or tsup --dts) and consume types from dist, not src. Avoids massive type-graph rebuilds.
rule.rationale: Importing types from src triggers recompilation of entire type graphs; consuming from dist breaks the dependency chain.

rule.statement: Hash-aware caching: list every input that affects build under inputs in turbo.json (e.g., *.css, prisma/schema.prisma); otherwise cache rot or false hits.
rule.rationale: Turborepo's hash ignores inputs outside the listed globs; missing inputs cause stale caches.

rule.statement: Pin Node + pnpm via engines + packageManager so agent runs and CI runs match local. Mismatched pnpm = lockfile churn.
rule.rationale: Version mismatch causes silent behavior differences and lockfile corruption.

rule.statement: Use Changesets for versioning even private packages — generates per-package CHANGELOGs that humans/agents can reason about.
rule.rationale: Per-package changelogs track who changed what and why, enabling better code review and release notes.

rule.statement: Don't hand-edit per-package tsconfigs; extend a single base. Agents diverge configs subtly per package, then types desync.
rule.rationale: Type desync across packages causes silent bugs where the same symbol compiles differently depending on the package.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Treat scaffolding as a one-shot task chain: (1) read this reference + project goals, (2) generate the directory tree, (3) install via pnpm install and verify turbo build --dry-run graph, (4) wire CI with cache tokens, (5) run the full pipeline once and inspect .turbo/ cache hits. For migrations, work package-by-package; never lift more than one repo per PR. Keep an apps/_template/packages/_template as the agent's reference for new packages.

template.purpose: Minimal turbo.json using tasks field (v2) with build/lint/typecheck/test/dev tasks and dependencies.
Strict shared base tsconfig with noUncheckedIndexedAccess and verbatimModuleSyntax for all packages.
GitHub Actions cache step for node_modules/.cache, .turbo, and .next/cache to speed up CI pipelines.
